{"problem_standardized_math":"Let $Q$ be a symmetric positive definite matrix, and let $\\lambda_1$ and $\\lambda_n$ denote the smallest and largest eigenvalues of $Q$, respectively. Prove that for every vector $x$,\n\\[\n\\frac{(x^T x)^2}{(x^T Qx)(x^T Q^{-1}x)} \\,\\ge\\, \\frac{4\\lambda_n\\lambda_1}{(\\lambda_n+\\lambda_1)^2}.\n\\]\nThis inequality is known as the Kantorovich inequality."}
